title: HOW TENNIS RACKETS HAVE CHANGED
content_html:
How Tennis Rackets Have Changed

In 2016, the British professional tennis player Andy Murray was ranked as the world's number one. It was an incredible achievement by any standard, made even more remarkable by the fact that he did this during a period considered to be one of the strongest in the sport's history, competing against the likes of Rafael Nadal, Roger Federer, and Novak Djokovic, to name just a few. Yet five years previously, he had been regarded as a talented outsider who entered but never won the major tournaments.

Of the changes that account for this transformation, one was visible and widely publicised: in 2011, Murray invited former number one player Ivan Lendl onto his coaching team - a valuable addition that had a visible impact on the player's playing style. Another change was so subtle as to pass more or less unnoticed. Like many players, Murray has long preferred a racket that consists of two types of string: one for the mains (verticals) and another for the crosses (horizontals). While he continued to use natural string in the crosses, in 2012 he switched to a synthetic string for the mains. A small change, perhaps, but its importance should not be underestimated.

The modification that Murray made is just one of a number of options available to players looking to tweak their rackets in order to improve their games. "Touring professionals have their rackets customised to their specific needs," says Colin Triplow, a UK-based professional racket stringer. "It's a highly important part of performance maximisation." Consequently, the specific rackets used by the world's elite are not readily available to the public; rather, each racket is individually made to suit the player who uses it. Take the US professional tennis players Mike and Bob Bryan, for example: "We're very particular with our racket specifications," they say. "All our rackets are sent from our manufacturer to Tampa, Florida, where our frames go through a thorough customisation process." They explain how they have adjusted not only racket length but even experimented with different kinds of paint. The rackets they use now weigh more than the average model and also have a denser string pattern (i.e., more crosses and mains).

The primary reason for these modifications is simple: as the line between winning and losing becomes thinner and thinner, even these slight changes become more and more important. As a result, players and their teams are becoming increasingly creative with the modifications to their rackets as they look to maximise their competitive advantage.

Racket modifications mainly date back to the 1970s when the amateur German tennis player Werner Fischer started playing with the so-called spaghetti-strung racket. It created a string bed that generated so much topspin that it was quickly banned by the International Tennis Federation. However, within a decade or two, racket modification became a regularity. Today it is, in many ways, an aspect of the game that is equal in significance to nutrition or training.

Modifications can be divided into two categories: those to the string bed and those to the racket frame. The former is far more common than the latter: the choice of the strings and the tension with which they are installed is something that nearly all professional players experiment with. They will continually change it depending on various factors including the court surface, climatic conditions, and game styles. Some will even change it depending on how they feel at the time.

At one time, all tennis rackets were strung with natural gut made from the outer layer of sheep or cow intestines. This all changed in the early 1990s with the development of synthetic strings that were cheaper and more durable. They are made from three materials: nylon (relatively durable and affordable), Kevlar (too stiff to be used alone), or co-polyester (polyester combined with additives that enhance its performance). Even so, many professional players continue to use a 'hybrid setup', where a combination of both synthetic and natural strings are used.

Of the synthetics, co-polyester is by far the most widely used. It's a perfect fit for the style of tennis now played, where players tend to battle it out from the back of the court rather than coming to the net. Studies indicate that the average spin from a co-polyester string is 25% greater than that from natural string or other synthetics. In a sense, the development of co-polyester strings has revolutionised the game.

However, many players go beyond these basic adjustments to the strings and make changes to the racket frame itself. For example, much of the serving power of US professional player Pete Sampras was attributed to the addition of four to five lead weights onto his rackets, and today many professionals have the weight adjusted during the manufacturing process.

Other changes to the frame involve the handle. Players have individual preferences for the shape of the handle and some will have the handle of one racket moulded onto the frame of a different racket. Other players make different changes. The professional Portuguese player Gonçalo Oliveira replaced the original grips of his rackets with something thinner because they had previously felt uncomfortable to hold.

Racket customisation and modification have pushed the standards of the game to greater levels that few could have anticipated in the days of natural strings and heavy wooden frames, and it's exciting to see what further developments there will be in the future.

questions.text: People had expected Andy Murray to become the world's top tennis player for at least five years before 2016.
questions.question: People had expected Andy Murray to become the world's top tennis player for at least five years before 2016.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 1–7 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“People had expected Andy Murray to become the world's top tennis player for at least five years before 2016.”

Đoạn:
“five years previously, he had been regarded as a talented outsider who entered but never won the major tournaments.”

Phân tích:

talented outsider ≠ expected future world number one
mọi người chưa thực sự tin anh sẽ thành số 1.

→ FALSE.

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: 1 People had expected Andy Murray to become world top tennis player 5 years before 2016.

questions.text: The change that Andy Murray made to his rackets attracted a lot of attention.
questions.question: The change that Andy Murray made to his rackets attracted a lot of attention.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“The change that Andy Murray made to his rackets attracted a lot of attention.”

Đoạn:
“Another change was so subtle as to pass more or less unnoticed.”

→ unnoticed = không được chú ý.

→ FALSE.
questions.question_text: 2 The change that Andy Murray made attracted a lot of attention.

questions.text: Most of the world's top players take a professional racket stringer on tour with them.
questions.question: Most of the world's top players take a professional racket stringer on tour with them.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“Most of the world's top players take a professional racket stringer on tour with them.”

Bài chỉ nhắc:
“Colin Triplow, a professional racket stringer”

Không có:

most players
take on tour

→ NOT GIVEN.
questions.question_text: 3 Most of the world's top players take professional racket stringer on tour.

questions.text: Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light in comparison to the majority of rackets.
questions.question: Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light in comparison to the majority of rackets.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light”

Đoạn:
“The rackets they use now weigh more than the average model”

→ nặng hơn average.

→ FALSE.
questions.question_text: 4 Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light compared to majority.

questions.text: Werner Fischer played with a spaghetti-strung racket that he designed himself.
questions.question: Werner Fischer played with a spaghetti-strung racket that he designed himself.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“Werner Fischer played with a spaghetti-strung racket that he designed himself.”

Bài chỉ nói:
“Werner Fischer started playing with the so-called spaghetti-strung racket.”

Không nói ông tự thiết kế.

→ NOT GIVEN.
questions.question_text: 5 Werner Fischer played with spaghetti-strung racket he designed himself.

questions.text: The weather can affect how professional players adjust the strings on their rackets.
questions.question: The weather can affect how professional players adjust the strings on their rackets.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“The weather can affect how professional players adjust the strings”

Đoạn:
“They will continually change it depending on ... climatic conditions”

→ climatic conditions = weather.

→ TRUE.
questions.question_text: 6 Weather can affect how professional players adjust strings.

questions.text: It was believed that the change Pete Sampras made to his rackets contributed to his strong serve.
questions.question: It was believed that the change Pete Sampras made to his rackets contributed to his strong serve.
questions.explanation:
Statement:
“It was believed that the change Pete Sampras made ... contributed to his strong serve.”

Đoạn:
“much of the serving power ... was attributed to the addition of four to five lead weights”

→ attributed to = believed caused by.

→ TRUE.
questions.question_text: 7 Believed change Pete Sampras made contributed to strong serve.

questions.text: Mike and Bob Bryan made changes to the types of ________     used on their racket frames.
questions.question: Mike and Bob Bryan made changes to the types of ________     used on their racket frames.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 8–13

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“changes to the types of ___ used on their racket frames”

Đoạn:
“experimented with different kinds of paint”

→ Đáp án: paint.

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**The tennis racket and how it has changed**
• Mike and Bob Bryan made changes to the types of [8] used on their racket frames.
• Players were not allowed to use the spaghetti-strung racket because of the amount of [9]  it created.
• Changes to rackets can be regarded as being as important as players' diets or the [10] they do.
• All rackets used to have natural strings made from the [11] of animals.
• Pete Sampras had metal [12] put into the frames of his rackets.
• Goncalo Oliveira changed the [13] on his racket handles.

questions.questionHtml:
The tennis racket and how it has changed

  • Mike and Bob Bryan made changes to the types of 8_______ used on their racket frames.
  • Players were not allowed to use the spaghetti-strung racket because of the amount of 9_______ it created.
  • Changes to rackets can be regarded as being as important as players' diets or the 10_______ they do.
  • All rackets used to have natural strings made from the 11_______ of animals.
  • Pete Sampras had metal 12_______ put into the frames of his rackets.
  • Goncalo Oliveira changed the 13_______ on his racket handles.
questions.question_text: 8 types of ___________ used on racket frames

questions.text: Players were not allowed to use the spaghetti-strung racket because of the amount of   ________ it created.
questions.question: Players were not allowed to use the spaghetti-strung racket because of the amount of   ________ it created.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“because of the amount of ___ it created”

Đoạn:
“generated so much topspin”

→ Đáp án: topspin.

questions.text: Changes to rackets can be regarded as being as important as players' diets or the _______ they do.
questions.question: Changes to rackets can be regarded as being as important as players' diets or the _______ they do.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“as important as players' diets or the ___ they do”

Đoạn:
“equal in significance to nutrition or training”

→ diets = nutrition.

→ Đáp án: training.
questions.question_text: 10 equal in significance to nutrition or ___________

questions.text:
All rackets used to have natural strings made from the 
_______of animals.
questions.question:
All rackets used to have natural strings made from the 
_______of animals.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“made from the ___ of animals”

Đoạn:
“made from the outer layer of sheep or cow intestines.”

→ Đáp án: intestines.
questions.question_text: 11 natural strings made from the ___________ of animals.

questions.text: Pete Sampras had metal ________put into the frames of his rackets.
questions.question: Pete Sampras had metal ________put into the frames of his rackets.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“Pete Sampras had metal ___ put into the frames”

Đoạn:
“addition of four to five lead weights”

→ metal weights.

→ Đáp án: weights.
questions.question_text: 12 Sampras had metal ___________ put into frames

questions.text: Goncalo Oliveira changed the_______ on his racket handles.
questions.question: Goncalo Oliveira changed the_______ on his racket handles.
questions.explanation:
Câu:
“Oliveira changed the ___ on his racket handles.”

Đoạn:
“replaced the original grips”

→ Đáp án: grips.
